beats.prompt: Quick recall вҖ” match each concept to what it does.

pairs.right: split a multi-step chance by where step 1 lands
apply one step, then total-probability over where you land

feedback.correct: A multi-step chance is not one product вҖ” you condition on the first step and sum over where you land. That's the law of total probability; net-new this lesson is that matrix powers do that summing automatically, at every step.
hints: Warm-up from Penney's first-step analysis and Bayes вҖ” no pressure.
A multi-step chance isn't a single path's product: split on the first step and add the cases.
First-step split = total probability = the move PвҒҝ automates.

beats.prompt: It rained today in the Land of Oz. Two days from now, what is the probability it is snowing?

options: 1/8 вҖ” it's the one story RainвҶ’NiceвҶ’Snow
1/4 вҖ” that's P(RainвҶ’Snow) in one day
3/8 вҖ” add up all three two-day paths

1/8 вҖ” it's the one story RainвҶ’NiceвҶ.note: RainвҶ’NiceвҶ’Snow is one way, worth 1/8 вҖ” but snow-in-two-days can also come RainвҶ’RainвҶ’Snow (1/8) and RainвҶ’SnowвҶ’Snow (1/8). It's all three: 1/8+1/8+1/8 = 3/8.

1/4 вҖ” that's P(RainвҶ’Snow) in one day.note: 1/4 is the ONE-step chance. Two days means an intermediate day you must pass through and sum over вҖ” that's PВІ, not P.

1/16 вҖ” square the one-step chance.note: (1/4)ВІ = 1/16 is 'square each entry'. But (PВІ)бөўвұј is rowВ·column вҖ” a sum of products, not a square.

3/8 вҖ” add up all three two-day paths.note: Right вҖ” sum the three RainвҶ’Snow paths (each 1/8) вҶ’ 3/8. (PвҒҝ)бөўвұј is the bookkeeping that never lets you drop a path.

hints: No wrong guess yet вҖ” commit and we'll trace the paths.
Snow in two days has more than one route вҖ” one path vs the sum.
Three paths, each 1/8 вҶ’ 3/8.

beats.prompt: Read this just-in-time primer on n-step probabilities and ChapmanвҖ“Kolmogorov вҖ” then continue.

interaction.title: n-step probabilities: PвҒҝ and ChapmanвҖ“Kolmogorov
interaction.body: The chance of going from state i to state j in exactly n steps is a single entry of the matrix power: (PвҒҝ)бөўвұј. It already adds up every length-n path from i to j, because matrix multiplication applies the law of total probability one step at a time. ChapmanвҖ“Kolmogorov names this: PбөҗвҒәвҒҝ = PбөҗВ·PвҒҝ вҖ” split an (m+n)-step trip at the intermediate state and sum over it.

feedback.correct: n-step transition probability = (PвҒҝ)бөўвұј. ChapmanвҖ“Kolmogorov: PбөҗвҒәвҒҝ = PбөҗВ·PвҒҝ.
hints: One step is P; n steps is PвҒҝ.
(PвҒҝ)бөўвұј already sums every length-n path iвҶ’j вҖ” that's ChapmanвҖ“Kolmogorov.
Compose by multiplying (PбөҗвҒәвҒҝ = Pбөҗ PвҒҝ), not by adding.

beats.prompt: Two-state weather: P(ClearвҶ’Clear) = 3/5, P(ClearвҶ’Rainy) = 2/5, P(RainyвҶ’Clear) = 3/10, P(RainyвҶ’Rainy) = 7/10. What is (PВІ)_{Clear,Clear} вҖ” the two-day chance of being Clear starting from Clear?

feedback.correct: (PВІ)cc = (3/5В·3/5) + (2/5В·3/10) = 9/25 + 3/25 = 12/25. Two ways to be clear in two days: stay clear, or detour through rainy.
hints: Two days ClearвҶ’Clear: list the ways the MIDDLE day could go.
(PВІ)cc isn't (3/5)ВІ = 9/25 вҖ” that's only ClearвҶ’ClearвҶ’Clear; you dropped the via-rainy path.
Add ClearвҶ’RainyвҶ’Clear (2/5В·3/10 = 3/25): 9/25 + 3/25 = 12/25.

beats.prompt: Step n from 1 to 2 and watch each cell of PВІ fill as a sum of path-products.

feedback.correct: Watch PВ№ become PВІ: each entry fills as a sum of path-products, and every row still sums to 1. The RainвҶ’Snow entry lands at 3/8 вҖ” the three two-day paths, added.
hints: Step n from 1 to 2 and watch the RainвҶ’Snow cell fill.
PвҒҝ вү  square each entry, and вү  P+P вҖ” you compose by MULTIPLYING (PВ·P); squaring or adding breaks the row-sum-to-1 law.
Each (PВІ)бөўвұј = row i В· column j; the RainвҶ’Snow cell sums to 3/8.

beats.prompt: Now compute it exactly: what is (PВІ)_{Rain,Snow} вҖ” the probability of being in Snow two days after Rain?

feedback.correct: Decompose by the middle day: RвҶ’RвҶ’S (ВҪВ·Вј) + RвҶ’NвҶ’S (ВјВ·ВҪ) + RвҶ’SвҶ’S (ВјВ·ВҪ) = 1/8+1/8+1/8 = 3/8. The intermediate state is exactly what you sum over.
hints: List the three middle days вҖ” the chain can pass through Rain, Nice, or Snow.
Don't stop at one path (1/8): RainвҶ’NiceвҶ’Snow is just one of three two-day routes to snow.

beats.prompt: The PВІ grid for Land of Oz is shown. Tap the RainвҶ’Rain cell to expand its path-products, then confirm the value.

feedback.correct: (PВІ)Rain,Rain = (ВҪВ·ВҪ) + (ВјВ·ВҪ) + (ВјВ·Вј) = 1/4 + 1/8 + 1/16 = 7/16. Even the diagonal is a path-sum, not just 'stay put' вҖ” and row Rain of PВІ = (7/16, 3/16, 3/8) sums to 1.
hints: Tap the RainвҶ’Rain cell of PВІ and read what it sums.
The 2-step diagonal isn't only RainвҶ’RainвҶ’Rain (1/4): add the come-back paths RainвҶ’NiceвҶ’Rain and RainвҶ’SnowвҶ’Rain.

beats.prompt: Reveal each of three lenses вҖ” they all converge on the same (PВІ)_{Rain,Snow}.

lenses.label: Sum over paths
lenses.body: RвҶ’RвҶ’S + RвҶ’NвҶ’S + RвҶ’SвҶ’S = ВҪВ·Вј + ВјВ·ВҪ + ВјВ·ВҪ = 1/8+1/8+1/8 = 3/8

lenses.label: Row Г— column
lenses.body: (PВІ)Rain,Snow = (row Rain of P)В·(col Snow of P) = [ВҪ,Вј,Вј]В·[Вј,ВҪ,ВҪ]бөҖ = 3/8

lenses.label: One step of total probability
lenses.body: P(Snow in 2 | Rain) = ОЈвӮ– P(RainвҶ’k)В·P(kвҶ’Snow) вҖ” condition on the middle day, then add (cf. bayes.ts sequentialPosterior)

beats.interviewNote: The n-step transition probability is the (i,j) entry of PвҒҝ вҖ” interviewers check that you sum over the intermediate state, not just multiply one path. (PВІ)бөўвұј = ОЈвӮ– pбөўвӮ–pвӮ–вұј is the law of total probability with the middle step as the cases.

feedback.correct: Sum-over-paths, the row Г— column matrix product, and one-step total probability are the same computation вҖ” all land on (PВІ)Rain,Snow = 3/8.
hints: Reveal each lens.
Matrix powers aren't a new trick вҖ” they're total probability, composed (the same move you used in Bayes).
All three roads give 3/8.

beats.prompt: Mastery challenge вҖ” same Land of Oz chain. It rained today. What is the probability it is snowing in three days?

interaction.scenario: Same Land of Oz chain. It **rained today**. Three days from now, what is the chance it is **snowing**? вҖ” i.e. \((P^3)_{\text{Rain},\text{Snow}}\).

feedback.correct: Let \(P^n\) do the summing: \[ (P^3)_{R,S} = (P^2 \cdot P)_{R,S} = \frac{7}{16}\cdot\frac{1}{4} + \frac{3}{16}\cdot\frac{1}{2} + \frac{6}{16}\cdot\frac{1}{2} = \frac{7}{64} + \frac{6}{64} + \frac{12}{64} = \frac{25}{64} \] The sum of all nine length-3 RainвҶ’Snow paths.
hints: Three days = one more step. Reuse the PВІ row for Rain you just found: (7/16, 3/16, 6/16).
Three steps isn't picking one 3-edge path вҖ” sum over BOTH intermediate days, or just do one more PВ·P.

beats.prompt: Reveal the recap to review the core principle.

feedback.correct: The chance of getting from i to j in n steps is one number вҖ” (PвҒҝ)бөўвұј вҖ” and it already sums every length-n path, because matrix multiplication is the law of total probability applied one step at a time.
hints: Two-day snow after rain is 3/8 вҖ” three paths of 1/8, not the single 1/8 story.
Every PвҒҝ entry is a rowВ·column path-sum; the rows always sum to 1.
Next up: which states does a chain return to forever, and which does it leave for good? Classifying states (recurrent, transient, absorbing).
